exam_name: Designing and Implementing a Data Science Solution on Azure

testing_centers.notes: Available at Certiport Authorized Testing Centers (primarily academic)

id_requirements: One valid, government-issued photo ID. Name on ID must match registration. For online: webcam required, room must be clear of people and materials.
exam_rules: No notes, books, or study materials. No phones or smartwatches. Whiteboard or scratch paper may be provided by testing center. Online: no dual monitors, no virtual machines, no headphones, must be alone in a closed room.
renewal_options: Microsoft certifications (Associate/Expert/Specialty) are renewed annually for free via a short renewal assessment on Microsoft Learn. Renewal assessment available 6 months before expiry. Fundamentals certifications do not expire.
target_audience: As a candidate for this exam, you should have subject matter expertise in applying data science and machine learning to implement and run machine learning workloads on Azure. Additionally, you should have knowledge of optimizing language models for AI applications using Azure AI.
recommended_experience: Knowledge and experience in data science by using: Azure Machine Learning, MLflow, Azure AI services, including Azure AI Search, Azure AI Foundry

prerequisites.description: Knowledge and experience in data science by using: Azure Machine Learning, MLflow, Azure AI services, including Azure AI Search, Azure AI Foundry

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period for first retake if score >= 500 on failed attempt. If score < 500: 14-day waiting period before retake. Maximum 5 attempts per exam per year (365 days). Free retake voucher sometimes included in official instructor-led training.

domains.name: Design and prepare a machine learning solution

objectives.title: Design a machine learning solution
sub_objectives: Identify the structure and format for datasets
Determine the compute specifications for machine learning workload
Select the development approach to train a model
Create and manage resources in an Azure Machine Learning workspace

objectives.title: Create and manage a workspace
sub_objectives: Create and manage datastores
Create and manage compute targets
Set up Git integration for source control
Create and manage assets in an Azure Machine Learning workspace

objectives.title: Create and manage data assets
sub_objectives: Create and manage environments
Share assets across workspaces by using registries

objectives.title: Use automated machine learning to explore optimal models
sub_objectives: Use automated machine learning for tabular data
Use automated machine learning for computer vision
Use automated machine learning for natural language processing

objectives.title: Select and understand training options
sub_objectives: Select and understand training options, including preprocessing and algorithms
Evaluate an automated machine learning run, including responsible AI guidelines

objectives.title: Use notebooks for custom model training
sub_objectives: Use the terminal to configure a compute instance
Access and wrangle data in notebooks
Wrangle data interactively with attached Synapse Spark pools and serverless Spark compute
Retrieve features from a feature store to train a model

objectives.title: Track model training by using MLflow
sub_objectives: Evaluate a model, including responsible AI guidelines
Automate hyperparameter tuning
Select a sampling method
Define the search space
Define the primary metric
Define early termination options

objectives.title: Run model training scripts
sub_objectives: Consume data in a job
Configure compute for a job run
Configure an environment for a job run
Track model training with MLflow in a job run
Define parameters for a job
Run a script as a job

objectives.title: Use logs to troubleshoot job run errors
Implement training pipelines
sub_objectives: Create custom components
Create a pipeline
Pass data between steps in a pipeline
Run and schedule a pipeline
Monitor and troubleshoot pipeline runs

objectives.title: Manage models
sub_objectives: Define the signature in the MLmodel file
Package a feature retrieval specification with the model artifact
Register an MLflow model
Assess a model by using responsible AI principles

objectives.title: Deploy a model
sub_objectives: Configure settings for online deployment
Deploy a model to an online endpoint
Test an online deployed service
Configure compute for a batch deployment
Deploy a model to a batch endpoint
Invoke the batch endpoint to start a batch scoring job

domains.name: Optimize language models for AI applications

objectives.title: Prepare for model optimization
sub_objectives: Select and deploy a language model from the model catalog
Compare language models using benchmarks
Test a deployed language model in the playground

objectives.title: Optimize through prompt engineering and prompt flow
sub_objectives: Test prompts with manual evaluation
Define and track prompt variants
Create prompt templates
Define chaining logic with the prompt flow SDK
Use tracing to evaluate your flow

objectives.title: Optimize through Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG)
sub_objectives: Prepare data for RAG, including cleaning, chunking, and embedding
Configure a vector store
Configure an Azure AI Search-based index store
Evaluate your RAG solution

objectives.title: Optimize through fine-tuning
sub_objectives: Prepare data for fine-tuning
Select an appropriate base model
Run a fine-tuning job
Evaluate your fine-tuned model

official_study_resources.title: Microsoft Learn (free self-paced training)
official_study_resources.notes: Free official learning paths mapped to each exam

official_study_resources.title: Microsoft Official Practice Assessment
official_study_resources.notes: Free official practice questions on Microsoft Learn

aliases: designing and implementing a data science solution on azure
